title: National Holocaust Monument Act
description: National Holocaust Monument Act (National Holocaust Monument Act). Current to 2026-02-04.

provisions.title: Short title
provisions.content: 1 This Act may be cited as the National Holocaust Monument Act .

provisions.title: Definitions
provisions.content: 2 The following definitions apply in this Act. Council Council means the National Holocaust Monument Development Council established by the Minister under section 4. ( Conseil ) Minister Minister means the Minister of Canadian Heritage. ( ministre ) Monument Monument means the National Holocaust Monument referred to in section 3. ( Monument ) public land public land means an area of land owned by the Crown that is accessible to the public at all times. ( terrain public ) Previous Version

provisions.title: Establishment of Holocaust Monument
provisions.content: 3 A National Holocaust Monument shall be established to commemorate the victims and Canadian survivors of the Holocaust.

provisions.title: Council to be established
provisions.content: 4 Within one year after the coming into force of this Act, the Minister shall establish a council, to be referred to as the National Holocaust Monument Development Council, composed of not more than five members.

provisions.title: Selection process
provisions.content: 5 (1) The Minister shall hold an open application process whereby members of the public who possess a strong interest in, connection to, or familiarity with the Holocaust may apply to the Minister to become Council members. (2) The members of the Council are not entitled to be paid any remuneration for acting as Council members. (3) The Council shall adopt by-laws to carry out its functions under this Act.

provisions.title: Design and location of Monument
provisions.content: 6 The Minister, in cooperation with the Council, shall (a) oversee the planning and design of the Monument; (b) choose a suitable area of public land in the National Capital Region for the Monument to be located; and (c) hold public consultations and take into account the recommendations of the public when making any decision under paragraph (a) or (b).

provisions.title: Construction of Monument
provisions.content: 7 (1) The Minister shall be responsible for the construction of the Monument. (2) The Council shall spearhead a fundraising campaign to cover the cost of constructing the Monument. Previous Version

provisions.title: Historic Sites and Monuments Board of Canada
provisions.content: 8 (1) The Historic Sites and Monuments Board of Canada may assist the Council in the performance of its functions under this Act. (2) Nothing shall prevent the Minister from contributing funds for the cost of planning, designing, contructing and installing the Monument. Previous Version

provisions.title: Three years
provisions.content: 9 The Monument shall be completed not later than three years after the day on which sufficient funds, as determined by the Minister, have been raised under subsection 7(2).

provisions.title: Report
provisions.content: 10 The Council shall submit an annual report to the Minister and the appropriate Committee of the House on its activities. The first report shall be submitted within 15 months after the day on which the Council is established under section 4.

provisions.title: Property of National Capital Commission
provisions.content: 11 (1) Once the Monument has been installed it becomes the property of the National Capital Commission. (2) Once the Monument has become the property of the National Capital Commission, the Council shall transfer to the National Capital Commission any funds remaining from its fundraising campaign. Minister means the Minister of Canadian Heritage. ( ministre )
Monument means the National Holocaust Monument referred to in section 3. ( Monument )
public land means an area of land owned by the Crown that is accessible to the public at all times. ( terrain public )
